content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates strong move-to-read matching and sequence integrity throughout. Turn 4 drops from cognitive content into body, refusing to take the bait of 'fix the critic.' Turn 6 names the war ('the critic, and then there's you immediately trying to handle the critic') — exactly the move the breakthrough requires. Turn 8 is a high-quality pattern interrupt naming the packaging behavior in real time, then pivots to the feeling that was about to surface. Turn 10 is textbook 'stay with it' — short, holds the moment, refuses to fill silence. Turn 12 catches the precise instant the critic interrupted the tenderness, demonstrating the mechanism rather than explaining it. Turn 14 offers voice-as-care quietly as a question, not a clever insight, allowing the client to find it. The 'what if the critic is actually right' in turn 6 is the one slightly riskier move — it courts the trap of arguing whether the critic is right — but the coach used it to expose the trap structure, and when the client deflected, the coach pivoted (turn 8) rather than doubling down. Timing follows the path: regulation/body → feeling → naming the war → reframe, with each step earned. Coach consistently holds silence and lets moves land.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Standard opening question to get content; neither strong nor weak."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Drops from story to body; refuses to engage the request for a technique. Good first move."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the war ('the critic, and then there's you immediately trying to handle the critic') — the central move this scenario requires. Second sentence is riskier but used as a probe, not a position."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Live pattern naming ('you've named six different frameworks') and redirect to the feeling that was about to surface. Excellent timing."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Stay there. Don't explain it. Don't name it.' Minimal, body-focused, holds the moment. Textbook stay-with-it."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the precise moment the critic interrupted; uses the interruption itself as the teaching, not a lecture about it."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Voice-as-care reframe offered as a quiet question, not a clever insight. Trusts the client to do something with it. Lands — client softens noticeably."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["apply-view-to-the-voice", "voice-as-care", "resistance", "voice-in-the-head"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "The 'what if the critic is right' move in turn 6 flirts with a trap but is salvaged by the coach pivoting in turn 8 rather than doubling down on truth-testing. Could have leaned more explicitly into 'what are you defending' but the body/feeling track chosen was equally valid."
}
